purpose: Evaluate whether the existing 14-day momentum top-4 entry top-6 exit shadow can avoid unnecessary daily equal-weight resets when signed membership is unchanged, without allowing unacceptable market-neutrality or gross-exposure drift.

signal.source: strictly closed 15-minute OKX bars resampled to complete 4-hour bars
signal.known_at: 04:00 UTC after the signal bar closes
signal.entry: 04:00 UTC open; 15-minute delayed execution is a frozen falsification only

execution_overlay.parent_membership_rule: top-4 entry top-6 exit momentum hysteresis
execution_overlay.baseline_rebalance: reset all active members to equal absolute 0.125 weight at every daily decision time
execution_overlay.primary_rebalance: reset all active members to equal absolute 0.125 weight only when the signed active membership changes; otherwise carry the drifted weights without trading
execution_overlay.membership_change_definition: any symbol enters, exits or changes signed side relative to the previous daily target membership
execution_overlay.first_entry: initialize the first complete portfolio at equal weights
execution_overlay.terminal_liquidation: close the actual drifted final holdings and include the associated one-way cost

costs.funding: actual archived OKX funding cashflows; adverse funding components doubled in the stress case

falsification.randomization: circularly shift the complete signed membership path against the original tradable daily entry times and rerun the same path-dependent execution rule

prohibitions: Do not introduce a drift threshold, periodic fallback rebalance, volatility trigger, symbol-specific rule or partial rebalance after outcomes are opened.
Do not change the momentum lookback, top-4 entry, top-6 exit, entry phase, holding period, universe, direction, costs or funding treatment after outcomes are opened.
Do not relax net-exposure or gross-exposure drift limits to rescue a failure.
Do not remove symbols or select favorable time segments.
Do not claim an independent Alpha family or assign a new H number.
Do not promote to formal A-tier from historical results.
Do not enable automatic ordering or position management.
